methodsAttempted.query: site:purdue.edu Purdue University generative AI policy academic integrity students artificial intelligence
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the formal Artificial Intelligence Use policy, central AI@Purdue guidance, Teaching and Learning guidance, Secure Purdue policy application material, and library publisher-policy material.

methodsAttempted.query: site:purdue.edu Purdue University generative AI teaching guidance syllabus AI tools
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the Office of the Vice Provost for Teaching and Learning AI guidance, a Teaching@Purdue syllabus module, Purdue AI Resources, and news items about draft or strategy material.

methodsAttempted.query: site:purdue.edu Purdue University ChatGPT policy artificial intelligence data privacy security
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the Secure Purdue AI policy application page, an IT news item about Copilot, a library guide, and the AI@Purdue toolkit.

Followed official links from the AI Use policy and AI@Purdue pages to the Secure Purdue supplemental material, AI Governance and Review page, Teaching and Learning guidance, and the Generative AI Toolkit.
The AI Governance and Review page linked an official May 2026 AI tools PDF. It was parsed for context but not used for claims because the HTML toolkit and policy pages supplied clearer text.

artifacts.summary: Official Purdue discovery located a formal AI Use policy, supplemental Secure Purdue policy application material, central AI governance/review material, Teaching and Learning AI guidance, and an official AI toolkit. Rejected sources were publisher-policy, duplicate syllabus-module, news/draft, general student-conduct, or course-specific material that did not support university-level AI-policy claims.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Application of the Artificial Intelligence (AI) Use Policy
Guidance on the Use of AI in Teaching and Learning

artifacts.claimText: Purdue's AI Use policy treats AI tools and university data used with them as IT Resources and Information Assets subject to Purdue acceptable-use and security/privacy policy controls.

citation.citationTitle: Purdue University AI Policy Tracker record
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: AI tools and the university data used with them are IT Resources and Information Assets, respectively, as defined within and subject to the Acceptable Use policy.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Purdue classifies AI tools and related university data under existing IT-resource and information-asset controls.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: Artificial Intelligence Use (VII.A.5)
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Purdue requires prior approval before Sensitive or Restricted Data is entered into AI tools, including training or testing AI tools.

artifacts.title: Purdue University AI Policy Staged Source Review
artifacts.summary: Staged Purdue University review based only on official Purdue-controlled sources. Candidate claims cover the formal AI Use policy, Secure Purdue/AI@Purdue governance review requirements, Teaching and Learning guidance for course-level AI authorization and AI detection, and AI toolkit privacy/tool-access notices. Claims remain agent-reviewed candidates and are not canonical public facts.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
No Purdue login-gated SharePoint forms or internal review records were accessed.
The May 2026 AI tools PDF was parsed only for context because the HTML policy and toolkit pages supplied clearer evidence for this bundle.
Course-specific syllabi, draft-news items, and publisher-policy library guide content were rejected for university-level policy claims.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
